message.content:
Run ALL of the following bash commands in the project directory C:\Users\User\citedby-ai and report EVERY result. This is a read-only audit. Do NOT modify any files.

**6.1 — Dangerous useEffect:**
```bash
cd /c/Users/User/citedby-ai
echo "=== useEffect SANS deps array ==="
grep -rn "useEffect(() =>" --include="*.tsx" --exclude-dir=node_modules . | grep -v "\[" | head -20
```

```bash
cd /c/Users/User/citedby-ai
echo "=== useEffect avec state setter dans deps ==="
grep -rn "useEffect(" --include="*.tsx" --exclude-dir=node_modules . -A 5 | grep "set.*])" | head -10
```

```bash
cd /c/Users/User/citedby-ai
echo "=== setInterval count ==="
grep -rn "setInterval" --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" --exclude-dir=node_modules .
echo "=== clearInterval count ==="
grep -rn "clearInterval" --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" --exclude-dir=node_modules .
```

**6.2 — Memory leaks:**
```bash
cd /c/Users/User/citedby-ai
echo "=== addEventListener count ==="
grep -rn "addEventListener" --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" --exclude-dir=node_modules . | wc -l
echo "=== removeEventListener count ==="
grep -rn "removeEventListener" --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" --exclude-dir=node_modules . | wc -l
```

```bash
cd /c/Users/User/citedby-ai
echo "=== addEventListener details ==="
grep -rn "addEventListener" --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" --exclude-dir=node_modules .
echo "---"
echo "=== removeEventListener details ==="
grep -rn "removeEventListener" --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" --exclude-dir=node_modules .
```

```bash
cd /c/Users/User/citedby-ai
echo "=== Observers ==="
grep -rn "subscribe\|observe\|IntersectionObserver\|ResizeObserver\|MutationObserver" --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" --exclude-dir=node_modules .
```

Report ALL results verbatim. Group by section (6.1, 6.2).

content.text:
The Bash tool is denied for this session. I need Bash permission to execute these grep/wc commands as requested. Let me explain what is happening:

**What I was trying to do:**

You asked me to run 6 specific bash commands that perform a read-only audit of the codebase using `grep`, `wc`, and `head`. These commands search for:

1. **6.1 -- Dangerous useEffect:** `useEffect` calls without dependency arrays, `useEffect` with state setters in deps, and `setInterval`/`clearInterval` balance.
2. **6.2 -- Memory leaks:** `addEventListener`/`removeEventListener` count and details, and observer patterns (`subscribe`, `IntersectionObserver`, `ResizeObserver`, `MutationObserver`).

**Why I need Bash permission:**

These commands involve piping (`|`), `wc -l` for counting, `head` for truncation, and multi-pattern `grep` with `--include`/`--exclude-dir` flags. The Bash tool is the only way to faithfully execute these compound shell commands and report the verbatim output as you requested.

**How to proceed:**

Please re-enable Bash access (or grant permission for this session) so I can run these read-only audit commands. Alternatively, if you can run them yourself in a terminal, I can help you analyze the output afterward.
